mechanism_narrative: A rising level of nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positively correlated with PAYEMS month-over-month changes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.5% signals sufficiently tight monetary policy that increases corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising retail sales and falling unemployment indicate strengthening aggregate demand without offsetting wage pressure, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns with Rank IC greater than 0.04 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is between 4.5% and 5.5%, month-over-month declines in the unemployment rate signal a tightening labor market that increases wage costs and compresses corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns; outside this band the wage-pressure channel is inactive.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is between 4.5% and 5.5%, month-over-month declines in UNRATE do not coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate top-line revenue and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth does not exhibit positive Rank IC with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A level of housing starts above 1,000 thousand units reflects strong residential investment and durable goods demand, which transmits into broad equity market gains via construction and consumer spending multipliers.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p < 0.05) when HOUST exceeds 1000 units over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or falling federal funds rate generates negative real policy rates that erode fixed-income competitiveness and reallocate capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The spread between CPIAUCSL year-over-year change and FEDFUNDS fails to predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC greater than 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production indicates improving factory utilization and operating leverage, which translates into margin expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not show positive correlation with S&P 500 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.
